entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume species being studied for nonnodulation genetics.

entities.name: NONNODULATION
entities.description: The inability of a plant to form root nodules, denoted as Nod-.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia by microorganisms.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics where chickpea genotypes were identified.

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The ability of a plant to form root nodules, denoted as Nod+.

entities.name: RHIZOBIA
entities.description: The soil bacteria used to inoculate plants to test for nodule formation.

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of introducing rhizobial strains to the plant roots.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where the inoculation and nodule tests were conducted.

entities.name: RECESSIVE GENE
entities.description: A gene that must be present in two copies to express the nonnodulation trait.

entities.name: GENETIC COMPLEMENTATION
entities.description: A technique used to determine if two mutations occur in the same gene.

entities.name: ALLELIC GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of mutations that are allelic to each other, meaning they affect the same gene.

entities.name: NONNODULATION GENES
entities.description: Specific genes identified as controlling the nonnodulation trait in chickpea.

entities.name: SEGREGATION RATIOS
entities.description: The proportions of offspring with specific phenotypes in genetic crosses.

entities.name: PM 233
entities.description: A specific nonnodulating chickpea line showing breakdown of the trait.

entities.name: ICC 5003 M
entities.description: A specific nonnodulating chickpea line showing breakdown of the trait.

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: The family of plants to which chickpea belongs, often used as reference plants for nitrogen fixation studies.
